snippets.title: ReLU, Leaky ReLU, and PReLU in NumPy
snippets.explanation: ReLU is element-wise max(0, x). Leaky and PReLU differ only in the negative branch: fixed α vs learned α per channel.

snippets.title: Built-in activations in PyTorch
snippets.code:
import torch
import torch.nn as nn

# Common patterns in nn.Sequential
model = nn.Sequential(
    nn.Linear(128, 64),
    nn.ReLU(inplace=True),           # standard ReLU
    nn.Linear(64, 32),
    nn.LeakyReLU(negative_slope=0.01),  # Leaky ReLU
    nn.Linear(32, 10),
    # output: softmax or cross-entropy with logits — not ReLU
)

# PReLU: one learnable alpha per input feature
prelu_layer = nn.PReLU(num_parameters=64)  # 64 channels/features
x = torch.randn(8, 64)
out = prelu_layer(x)
print("PReLU alphas:", prelu_layer.weight.data[:4])
snippets.explanation: `nn.ReLU` and `nn.LeakyReLU` are drop-in after linear/conv layers. `nn.PReLU` exposes learnable negative slopes—check `num_parameters` matches channel count.

snippets.title: Detecting dying ReLU units in a forward pass
snippets.code:
import torch
import torch.nn as nn

layer = nn.Linear(4, 6)
relu = nn.ReLU()
X = torch.randn(32, 4)  # batch of 32

with torch.no_grad():
    z = layer(X)
    a = relu(z)
    # fraction of activations that are exactly zero
    dead_frac = (a == 0).float().mean().item()
    # neurons never firing across the batch
    dead_neurons = (a.sum(dim=0) == 0).nonzero().flatten().tolist()
    print(f"dead activation fraction: {dead_frac:.2%}")
    print(f"completely dead neuron indices: {dead_neurons}")
snippets.explanation: Track how many activations are zero and which neurons never fire on a batch—early warning for dying ReLU before accuracy collapses.
